messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
last team to win fa cup outside top flight

content.message:
I need to find the last team to win the FA Cup outside the top flight. Let me break this down step by step:

1. First, I need to understand what "outside top flight" means - this refers to teams from non-premier league (non-top flight) English football leagues.
2. I need to identify the FA Cup winners throughout history, specifically focusing on teams that were not in the top flight.
3. I need to determine the most recent (last) such team.

Let me start by searching for information about the FA Cup and its winners.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. AEK Football player ID (wd:P13894 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "football.aek.com"), also known as "football.aek.com":
  - Identifier for football players in AEK database (football.aek.com).
2. Opta football competition ID (wd:P8735 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "Opta football cup ID"), also known as "Opta competition ID", "Opta cup ID", "Opta football cup ID", "Opta football league ID", "Opta league ID", etc.:
  - identifier of a football competition in Opta
3. Sport Express football match ID (wd:P13569 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "S-E football match ID", "SE football match ID", "Sport-Express football match ID":
  - identifier for a association football (soccer) match in the Sport Express database
4. UEFA player ID (wd:P2276 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "uefa.com/"), also known as "https://www.uefa.com/", "https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/clubs/players/", "uefa.com/", "UEFA ID", "www.uefa.com/":
  - identifier for UEFA players
5. FootballDatabase.eu person ID (wd:P3537 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "footballdatabase.eu/"), also known as "footballdatabase.eu/", "FootballDatabase.eu ID", "https://www.footballdatabase.eu/", "https://www.footballdatabase.eu/en/", "https://www.footballdatabase.eu/en/player/", etc.:
  - identifier for a person at FootballDatabase.eu
6. Football Money League rank (wd:P6697 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "Deloitte Football Rank":
  - rank in the Deloitte Football Money League
7. Soccerbase player ID (wd:P2193 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "soccerbase.com/"), also known as "https://www.soccerbase.com/", "https://www.soccerbase.com/players/", "soccerbase.com/", "www.soccerbase.com/":
  - player ID on the Soccerbase.com website
8. league or competition (wd:P118 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "tournament"), also known as "competition", "cup", "division", "sports league", "tournament":
  - league or competition in which team or player has played, or in which an event occurs
9. Championat ID (wd:P10800 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "championat.com/"), also known as "championat.com/", "Championat.com ID", "Championat.com identifier", "Championat identifier", "https://www.championat.com/", etc.:
  - identifier of a team, sports facility, or player on the Championat website
10. UEFA stadium category (wd:P9803 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - Category of the sports facility according to the UEFA Stadium Infrastructure Regulations

content.message: I need to find information about the FA Cup winners, specifically those from outside the top flight. Let me search for information about FA Cup winners.

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. country of citizenship (wd:P27 as wdt/p, matched via "nation"), also known as "citizen of", "citizenship", "country", "(legal) nationality", "nation", etc.:
  - the object is a country that recognizes the subject as its citizen
2. instance of (wd:P31 as wdt/p, matched via "is a"), also known as "example of", "has type", "is a", "is an", "is of type", etc.:
  - type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different ... is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain
3. given name (wd:P735 as wdt/p, matched via "name"), also known as "Christian name", "first name", "firstname", "forename", "middle name", etc.:
  - first name or another given name of this person; values used with the property should not link disambiguations nor family names
4. image (wd:P18 as wdt/p, matched via "still"), also known as "album artwork", "album cover", "artwork", "camera picture", "Commons image", etc.:
  - image of relevant illustration of the subject; if available, ... map, flag image, signature image, logo image, collage image)
5. occupation (wd:P106 as wdt/p, matched via "employ"), also known as "avocation", "career", "craft", "employ", "employment", etc.:
  - occupation of a person. See also "field of work" ... (Property:P452), "members have occupation" (Property:P3989).
6. sex or gender (wd:P21 as wdt/p, matched via "gender"), also known as "biological sex", "gender", "gender expression", "gender identity", "gender or sex", etc.:
  - sex or gender identity of human or animal. For human: male, ... female organism. Groups of same gender use subclass of (P279)
7. place of birth (wd:P19 as wdt/p, matched via "P19"), also known as "birth city", "birth location", "birth place", "birthplace", "born", etc.:
  - most specific known birth location of a person, animal or fictional character
8. Freebase ID (wd:P646 as wdt/p, matched via "MID"), also known as "FID", "Freebase", "Freebase identifier", "MID":
  - identifier for a page in the Freebase database. Format: ... starting with "/g/", use Google Knowledge Graph ID (P2671)
9. official website (wd:P856 as wdt/p, matched via "website (official)"), also known as "home page", "homepage", "home page (official)", "homepage (official)", "official homepage", etc.:
  - URL of the official page of an item (current or former). ... see the "Hijacked or dead websites" section of the Talk page
10. date of birth (wd:P569 as wdt/p, matched via "born"), also known as "birth date", "birthdate", "birth year", "birthyear", "born", etc.:
  - date on which the subject was born

content.message: I need to find information about FA Cup winners. Let me search for properties related to FA Cup winners directly.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. competition won (wd:P2522 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "contest won"), also known as "contest won", "event won", "winner of", "won", "won event":
  - competition or sports event won by the subject
2. award received (wd:P166 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "winner of"), also known as "award", "awarded", "awards", "awards received", "award won", etc.:
  - award or recognition received by a person, organization or creative work
3. AEK Football player ID (wd:P13894 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "football.aek.com"), also known as "football.aek.com":
  - Identifier for football players in AEK database (football.aek.com).
4. points for (wd:P1358 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "championship points"), also known as "championship points", "competition points", "league points", "pf", "points", etc.:
  - number of points in a league table or decathlon. (Use P1351 for goals/points in a match)
5. Football Money League rank (wd:P6697 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "Deloitte Football Rank":
  - rank in the Deloitte Football Money League
6. Championat ID (wd:P10800 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "championat.com/"), also known as "championat.com/", "Championat.com ID", "Championat.com identifier", "Championat identifier", "https://www.championat.com/", etc.:
  - identifier of a team, sports facility, or player on the Championat website
7. Opta football competition ID (wd:P8735 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "Opta football cup ID"), also known as "Opta competition ID", "Opta cup ID", "Opta football cup ID", "Opta football league ID", "Opta league ID", etc.:
  - identifier of a football competition in Opta
8. winner (wd:P1346 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "champion"), also known as "1st place medalist", "awardee", "champ", "champion", "has winner", etc.:
  - winner of a competition or similar event, not to be used from ... qualified with "for work" (P1686)) nor for wars or battles
9. England Football Online manager ID (wd:P6330 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "England Football Online manager profile"), also known as "England Football Online manager profile":
  - identifier for football manager on England Football Online site
10. Cambridge University Press book ID (wd:P12847 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "CUP ID"), also known as "Cambridge University Press ID", "CUP ID":
  - identifier of a book from Cambridge University Press
